engineCodes.h2: Alfa Romeo Engine Codes & Average Rebuilt Prices (UK Supply)

All prices are typical UK market ranges for professionally rebuilt engines with warranty. Click "Get Quote" on any engine to receive tailored prices from our network of UK Alfa Romeo specialists.

groups.name: JTDM/JTD Diesel Family
groups.failureNote: JTDM/JTD engines are known for turbocharger wear, injector coking and EGR valve failures. Our rebuilt units include upgraded turbo seals, reconditioned injectors and cleaned EGR systems to restore reliability.

groups.name: MultiAir/TB Petrol Family
groups.failureNote: MultiAir/TB engines are known for timing chain tensioner wear and oil pump drive issues. Our rebuilt units include upgraded tensioners, revised oil pump components and full MultiAir unit calibration.

groups.name: V6 Performance Family
groups.failureNote: V6 performance engines are known for oil starvation under high load and timing chain guide wear. Our specialist rebuilds include reinforced guides, upgraded oil pumps and performance-spec bearings.

groups.name: Legacy TS/JTS Petrol Family
groups.failureNote: Legacy TS/JTS engines are known for timing belt failure and oil consumption issues. Our rebuilt units include new timing kits, revised piston rings and upgraded valve stem seals

commonProblems.h2: Common Alfa Romeo Engine Problems — What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: Alfa Romeo’s performance-focused powertrains deliver exceptional driveability, but premium engineering demands precise maintenance. Across the UK, replacement demand is dominated by the 1.4 TB MultiAir petrol, the 2.0 JTDm/JTD diesel families, and the newer 2.0 GME T4 units, which frequently present owners with a critical repair-or-replace decision. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components specifically designed to address these original weak points — plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty and nationwide supply-and-fit delivery. The following decision tool breaks down exact repair tiers, realistic UK pricing, and specialist verdicts to help you choose the most cost-effective path forward.

problems.h4: Timing Chain Failure – Why 1.4 MultiAir Alfa Romeo Engines Fail Prematurely
problems.rootCause: Factory plastic chain guides and low-preload hydraulic tensioners degrade under heat and extended oil change intervals. As the chain stretches, timing accuracy drops, leading to misfires and eventual valve contact.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: | Hydraulic tensioner swap, plastic guide kit, updated timing tools, fresh oil service | Viable only before metallic rattling or check-engine lights appear. Extends life by 30k–40k miles if caught early.

repairOptions.tier: | Intermediate – Full Timing Assembly & VVT Phaser Repair
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: | Chain replacement, upgraded guides/tensioner, VVT phaser inspection, sump flush, cam timing recalibration | Suitable if timing has slipped but cylinder pressure and valves remain intact. Requires meticulous assembly.

| Supply & fit of reconditioned unit with upgraded metal guides, revised tensioner, bench-tested, 12-month warranty | Best when debris has circulated or pistons/valves show wear. Eliminates recurrence risk and restores factory performance.

problems.recommendation: If you hear a light rattle on cold starts below 50,000 miles, a tensioner and guide kit is a cost-effective intervention. However, once the chain has jumped teeth or metallic particles enter the oil circuit, partial repairs become a false economy. For a car with strong residual value, a specialist-rebuilt 198 A4.000 unit with upgraded internals is the most reliable long-term fix.
problems.cta: → Compare rebuilt 1.4 MultiAir engine prices for your Giulietta or Mito

problems.h4: Turbo & Injector Failure – Diagnosing 2.0 JTDm Alfa Romeo Engine Problems
problems.affectedModels: Alfa Romeo Giulia, Giulietta, Stelvio (2015–present, 552 83 867 / 940 A2.000)
problems.rootCause: High-pressure common rail systems combined with aggressive EGR/DPF cycling cause carbon buildup and fuel dilution. Turbo oil feed lines crack or clog, starving the bearings and triggering compressor/seal failure.

repairOptions.tier: | Minor – Turbo Feed/Return Line & Intake Service
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: | Replace braided turbo oil lines, clean EGR/DPF assembly, intake decarbonisation, updated breather filters | Only effective when power loss is mild and turbo shaft play is minimal. Prevents immediate catastrophic failure.

| New turbo unit, injector leak-off testing/coding, DPF regeneration, high-pressure pump inspection | Suitable for isolated turbo failure with healthy bottom end. Requires precise calibration to prevent repeat strain.

| Supply & fit of warranty-backed recon unit with upgraded turbo feed system, cleaned injectors, dyno-tested, 12-month warranty | Ideal when fuel wash has scored cylinders or multiple systems have degraded simultaneously. Restores torque and efficiency.

problems.recommendation: 2.0 JTDM turbo failure rarely happens in isolation; it’s usually the symptom of prolonged oil starvation and fuel contamination. If caught early, a line replacement and turbo swap can save the block. Once black smoke, metallic whine, or limp mode becomes persistent, a full specialist rebuild with upgraded oil routing offers far greater peace of mind and matches OEM output.
problems.cta: → Get tailored quotes for rebuilt 2.0 JTDm engines with supply & fit

problems.h4: Cambelt Fracture & Hydrolock – When Alfa Romeo Engine Repair or Replace Becomes Critical
problems.rootCause: These are interference engines; any belt slip, water pump seizure, or tensioner collapse allows pistons to strike open valves. Coolant ingress from degraded housing seals can cause immediate hydrolock and bent connecting rods.

repairOptions.tier: | Intermediate – Cylinder Head Rebuild & Timing Reset
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: | Head removal, valve replacement, resurfacing, new belt/pump assembly, compression testing | Only viable if block integrity is confirmed and rods are straight. High labour cost with risk of hidden micro-cracks.

| Supply & fit of recon engine with new belt routing components, hydrostatic block test, bench-verified, 12-month warranty | Recommended after any belt snap or coolant ingestion. Eliminates structural uncertainty and returns vehicle to pre-failure reliability.

problems.recommendation: There is no middle ground once a cambelt snaps on these interference diesels. Head rebuilds are labour-intensive and carry a significant risk of undetected crankcase or coolant passage damage. For 159/Brera owners, a warranty-backed replacement engine with a fully revised timing system is consistently the most financially sound and reliable route.
problems.cta: → Compare hydrolock-safe recon engine prices for your 159, Brera or Spider

problems.h4: Cold Start Rattle & Chain Stretch – Protecting Your 2.0 GME Alfa Romeo Engine
problems.rootCause: Early-production hydraulic tensioners lack consistent preload, and direct-injection carbon accumulation increases valvetrain load. Combined with a sensitive PCV system, oil vapour bypass leads to sludge and premature chain stretch.

repairOptions.tier: | Minor – PCV & Breather Service + Tensioner Inspection
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: | Replace PCV valve, clean breather hoses, inspect tensioner preload, fresh synthetic oil flush | Effective if rattle is purely oil-pressure related at startup. Prevents sludge from accelerating chain wear.

repairOptions.tier: | Intermediate – Full Timing Chain & Tensioner Assembly
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: | Chain, guides, revised tensioner, cam phaser inspection, oil pump recalibration, ECU timing adaptation | Suitable when stretch is confirmed via diagnostic codes but cylinder compression remains healthy.

| Supply & fit of remanufactured unit with upgraded tensioners, reinforced PCV routing, dyno-tested, 12-month warranty | Best choice for persistent knocking, oil loss >1L/1000km, or cam timing deviation. Guarantees long-term performance compliance.

problems.recommendation: Early cold-start rattling on the 552 73 835 unit is almost always tensioner-related and responds well to a PCV service and tensioner upgrade. If the noise persists warm or triggers cam/crank correlation faults, the chain has stretched beyond safe limits. At this stage, a specialist rebuild with factory-uprated timing components is the only way to protect your Giulia or Stelvio’s long-term value.
problems.cta: → Request live quotes for rebuilt 2.0 GME engines with upgraded timing components

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Alfa Romeo
finalCta.paragraph: Engine failures on Alfa Romeo models rarely require full vehicle write-offs if you act before secondary damage spreads. EngineMarket removes the guesswork by letting you instantly compare supply-and-fit prices from trusted UK Alfa Romeo engine specialists. Every recon or remanufactured unit in our network features upgraded components targeting these exact failure points, undergoes rigorous bench testing, and arrives with a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty and nationwide recovery options. Compare transparent quotes today and keep your car on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Alfa Romeo Engine Replacement Prices Now
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Alfa Romeo model and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty — always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineTypes.h2: Alfa Romeo Engine Types – Options, Costs & What to Expect
engineTypes.intro: Every Alfa Romeo engine type available through our UK network, with typical price ranges and recommendations based on your vehicle's age, condition and your budget.

types.title: Alfa Romeo Reconditioned Engines (Recon)
types.description: A stripped, cleaned and fully inspected engine where all worn components are replaced with OEM‑spec parts. Best for owners who want factory reliability without the factory price — particularly effective for addressing common JTDM turbo wear or MultiAir timing chain concerns.
types.priceRange: Typical price range: £1,200 – £4,800 (depending on engine code)
types.cta: Compare reconditioned Alfa Romeo engine prices

types.title: Alfa Romeo Rebuilt Engines
types.description: A complete rebuild with upgraded components addressing known failure points — such as reinforced MultiAir timing chain tensioners, uprated JTDM injector nozzles and revised oil pump drives — for a longer service life than a standard replacement.
types.cta: Get rebuilt Alfa Romeo engine quotes

types.title: Used Alfa Romeo Engines
types.description: A tested, low‑mileage take‑out unit from a donor vehicle. The most affordable option when budget is the priority, with verified history and warranty available — ideal for older 147, 156 or GT models where a full rebuild isn't economical.
types.cta: Check used Alfa Romeo engine availability

types.title: Alfa Romeo Remanufactured Engines
types.description: A factory‑spec, as‑new engine: every surface is machined, every component is replaced, and the unit is built to meet exact OEM tolerances. The premium choice for Giulia Quadrifoglio, Stelvio or performance models where zero‑mile reliability is essential.
types.cta: Compare remanufactured Alfa Romeo engine costs

types.title: Alfa Romeo Refurbished Engines
types.description: A lightly serviced, cleaned, and tested engine that works reliably but hasn't had a complete rebuild. Often a pragmatic, fast solution for older or budget‑sensitive models like Mito or early Giulietta where a full recon isn't economical.
types.cta: View refurbished Alfa Romeo engine options

types.title: Alfa Romeo Supply & Fit Engines
types.description: Your replacement engine delivered and professionally installed by a UK Alfa Romeo specialist. Includes removal of the old unit, fitting, fluids, and a workmanship warranty — removing the hassle of sourcing a separate garage.
types.priceRange: Typical added cost: £400 – £1,200 on top of engine price
types.cta: Find Alfa Romeo engine supply & fit deals

engineTypes.closing: All engine types include a minimum 12‑month warranty. Prices are typical UK market ranges and depend on exact engine code, supplier, and any additional fitting services. Use our free comparison tool to get a tailored quote for your Alfa Romeo model today.

engineSizes.h2: Alfa Romeo Engines by Size – Find the Right Displacement for Your Model

items.title: Alfa Romeo 1.3L Diesel Engines
items.description: Compact and highly efficient, this MultiJet unit was widely fitted to Alfa’s smallest UK hatchbacks and superminis from the mid‑2000s through the 2010s. While known for excellent economy, high-mileage examples frequently require replacement due to turbo actuator fatigue and dual‑mass flywheel degradation that accelerates drivetrain vibration
items.cta: Get quotes for Alfa Romeo 1.3L diesel engines

items.title: Alfa Romeo 1.6L Diesel Engines
items.description: Introduced to bridge the gap between small-city efficiency and mid-size torque, this 1.6 JTDM powerplant powered the Giulietta and late-model 147/159 ranges. Owners typically seek replacement units once DPF saturation becomes chronic and high-pressure fuel delivery drops below acceptable thresholds
items.cta: Get quotes for Alfa Romeo 1.6L diesel engines

items.title: Alfa Romeo 1.9L Diesel Engines
items.description: One of the most celebrated diesel architectures in the Alfa lineup, the 1.9 JTD delivered robust low-end torque across the 147, 156, GT, and Brera platforms. As these vehicles age, turbo boost hose degradation, timing belt tensioner collapse, and injector pump wear routinely push owners toward specialist replacement rather than piecemeal repair
items.cta: Get quotes for Alfa Romeo 1.9L diesel engines

items.title: Alfa Romeo 2.0L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 2.0 JTDM/MultiJet II replaced the 1.9L as Alfa’s core diesel offering, featuring advanced common-rail delivery and broader torque curves for Giulia, Stelvio, and late Giulietta models. Replacement demand is primarily driven by turbo oil feed line fractures that starve the bearings and subsequent high-pressure fuel pump wear
items.cta: Get quotes for Alfa Romeo 2.0L diesel engines

items.title: Alfa Romeo 2.2L Diesel Engines
items.description: Positioned for heavier SUVs and executive sedans, the 2.2 JTDM delivers strong towing capability and refined highway cruising across Giulia, Stelvio, and Brera variants. Chronic replacement requests stem from cam phaser hydraulic leakage, injector calibration drift, and turbo variable vane seizure after extended urban use
items.cta: Get quotes for Alfa Romeo 2.2L diesel engines

items.title: Alfa Romeo 1.4L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.4 TB MultiAir turbo became the volume petrol workhorse for Giulietta and Mito, blending lightweight efficiency with punchy mid-range response. However, premature plastic timing guide disintegration and MultiAir electrohydraulic solenoid oil leaks make these units frequent candidates for warranty-backed recon replacements
items.cta: Compare Alfa Romeo 1.4L petrol engine prices

items.title: Alfa Romeo 1.6L Petrol Engines
items.description: A naturally aspirated Twin Spark unit known for smooth throttle response and distinctive Alfa exhaust note, this engine powered the 147, 156, and early Spider ranges. Replacement is typically triggered by Twin Spark coil pack degradation, variator gear wear, and persistent valve cover oil seepage that compromises ignition stability
items.cta: Compare Alfa Romeo 1.6L petrol engine prices

items.title: Alfa Romeo 1.75L Petrol Engines
items.description: Developed for high-performance applications, the 1.75 TBi powered the Giulietta Cloverleaf, Brera, and 159 Turbo, offering rapid acceleration with direct injection precision. Over time, intake valve carbon accumulation, piston ring land deposits, and turbo wastegate linkage wear frequently justify a full specialist swap
items.cta: Compare Alfa Romeo 1.75L petrol engine prices

items.title: Alfa Romeo 1.8L Petrol Engines
items.description: A refined evolution of the Twin Spark architecture, the 1.8 provided balanced power for the 147, 156, and Brera ranges across the late 1990s and 2000s. Owners commonly seek replacement after variator timing gear failure, thermostat housing stress fractures, and head gasket fatigue under sustained high-load driving
items.cta: Compare Alfa Romeo 1.8L petrol engine prices
commonFailurePoints: Twin spark variator timing gear failure

items.title: Alfa Romeo 2.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: Spanning Twin Spark, JTS direct injection, and the modern GME T4 platforms, this displacement has anchored Alfa’s performance petrol lineup for over two decades. Replacement demand concentrates around JTS intake valve carbonisation, early GME timing tensioner weakness, and oil consumption from aged piston rings
items.cta: Compare Alfa Romeo 2.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: Alfa Romeo 2.2L Petrol Engines
items.description: A direct-injection JTS unit shared with select mid-2000s performance coupes and sedans, delivering strong mid-range torque with a sporty exhaust character. High-pressure fuel pump seal degradation, excessive oil consumption from worn cylinder liners, and timing guide attrition routinely push these engines into replacement territory
items.cta: Compare Alfa Romeo 2.2L petrol engine prices

items.title: Alfa Romeo 3.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The legendary Busso V6 remains one of Alfa’s most sought-after powertrains, celebrated for its metallic exhaust note and linear power delivery across the 147, 156, GTV, and Spider ranges. Replacement is typically required when the oil pump drive gear shears under thermal stress or rear main seal failure causes critical lubrication loss
items.cta: Compare Alfa Romeo 3.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: Alfa Romeo 3.2L Petrol Engines
items.description: A modern V6 developed for Brera, Spider, and late 159 models, offering smooth refinement and strong highway overtaking capability. Timing chain tensioner collapse, variable intake manifold actuator failure, and head gasket stress under aggressive use are the primary drivers for specialist recon swaps
items.cta: Compare Alfa Romeo 3.2L petrol engine prices

groups.title: Alfa Romeo Hybrid & Electric Powertrains (if applicable)

items.title: Alfa Romeo 1.5L Hybrid Engines
items.description: Introduced with the Tonale, this plug-in/mild hybrid system pairs a turbocharged petrol block with an integrated electric motor and 48V battery architecture to deliver urban EV capability and improved fuel economy. Replacement is highly specialist-driven, with failures typically involving inverter cooling degradation or hybrid battery cell imbalance rather than mechanical wear. System / motor code(s): PHEV4e, 48V MHEV architecture variants
items.cta: Enquire about Alfa Romeo 1.5L hybrid engine availability

engineSizes.closing: | Alfa Romeo engine warranty | Closing line

fuelTypes.h2: Alfa Romeo Engines by Fuel Type
fuelTypes.intro: Choose your Alfa Romeo engine by fuel type to compare realistic replacement prices, known brand‑wide issues, and UK specialist supply – from the most popular diesel workhorses to the latest electrified powertrains.

items.title: Alfa Romeo Diesel Engines
items.description: Alfa Romeo diesel engines cover many of the brand's highest-mileage UK models. Replacement demand is usually driven by timing system wear, injector and turbo faults, plus EGR or DPF-related damage once repeat short-journey use starts affecting reliability.
knownFor: Dual-mass flywheel degradation
turbo vane actuator sticking
injector seal carbon buildup
DPF blockage & regeneration failure
importantNotes: Short-journey use increases DPF, EGR, and oil-dilution risk on many diesel engines.
A documented service history matters more on diesel replacements because injector, turbo, and timing faults often build gradually.
items.cta: Compare Alfa Romeo diesel engine prices

items.title: Alfa Romeo Petrol Engines
items.description: Alfa Romeo petrol engines span everything from core daily-driver models to higher-output performance variants. Most replacement enquiries start with timing chain noise, oil consumption, cooling-system failure, or internal wear that makes a warranted replacement more sensible than repeated repairs.
knownFor: Plastic timing guide disintegration
MultiAir solenoid oil leaks
turbo wastegate play
Twin spark coil pack failure
importantNotes: Cold-start rattles, overheating history, and oil use are the biggest warning signs before a petrol engine replacement becomes necessary.
Direct-injection petrol engines often need specialist attention for carbon build-up alongside the main mechanical repair.
items.cta: Compare Alfa Romeo petrol engine prices

fuelTypes.closing: Not sure which fuel type your Alfa Romeo has? Enter your registration number above to instantly identify your model, engine code, fuel system and compatible replacement options – all backed by a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty from UK Alfa Romeo specialists.

engineCodeDirectory.h2: Alfa Romeo Engine Codes – Most Replaced Engines (2008–2025)
engineCodeDirectory.intro: Every major Alfa Romeo engine code from the last two decades, grouped by family, with compatible models and typical reasons why owners seek replacement. Select your engine to compare rebuilt prices and get quotes from UK Alfa Romeo specialists.

entries.title: 552 73 835 2.0L Petrol
entries.description: Found exclusively in Giulia (952) and Stelvio (949) models from 2016 onward. High-pressure injection and aggressive turbo tuning frequently cause timing chain stretch and premature oil pump gear wear under daily driving. Quality rebuilt units feature reinforced chain guides, a revised high-flow oil pump assembly, and updated ECU thermal mapping for long-term reliability.
entries.cta: Compare quotes for 552 73 835 engine replacement

entries.title: 198 A4.000 1.4L Petrol
entries.description: Powers the Mito (955) and Giulietta (940) from 2009 to 2018. The hydraulic MultiAir valve system commonly suffers solenoid pack leakage, while the timing chain tensioner loses pressure around 70,000 miles. Reconditioned examples include upgraded solenoid seals, a heavy-duty tensioner, and precision-calibrated MultiAir control modules.
entries.cta: Get quotes for 198 A4.000 Alfa Romeo engine

entries.title: 940 A2.000 1.4L Petrol
entries.description: Fitted to mid-spec Giulietta (940) variants between 2010 and 2018. Owners frequently report cold-start rattling from chain guide wear and intermittent misfires from carbon-fouled injectors. Specialist rebuilds address this with metal-reinforced chain guides, ultrasonic-cleaned fuel rails, and revised piston crown geometry.
entries.cta: Find rebuilt 940 A2.000 engine prices

entries.title: 940 B7.000 1.7L Petrol
entries.description: The performance 1750 TBi unit used in the 4C (960) and high-output Giulietta (940) from 2013 to 2020. Track-focused driving accelerates turbo actuator fatigue and high-pressure fuel pump degradation. Upgraded replacement engines feature reinforced actuator linkages, forged pump internals, and improved crankcase ventilation.
entries.cta: Request quotes for 940 B7.000 performance engine

entries.title: 940 A1.000 1.4L Petrol
entries.description: Standard-fit engine across entry Mito (955) and early Giulietta (940) models (2008–2015). Known for turbo wastegate rattle and intercooler hose splitting under boost pressure. Rebuilt specifications include a metal-wastegate replacement, silicone intercooler couplers, and upgraded valve train clearances.
entries.cta: Check availability for 940 A1.000 replacement engine

entries.title: 939 A4.000 2.0L Diesel
entries.description: Widespread across Giulietta (940), 159 (939), and Brera (939) from 2008 to 2018. Injector tip coking and variable-geometry turbo actuator seizure are the primary failure drivers beyond 90,000 miles. Reconditioned units come with recalibrated piezo injectors, ceramic-coated turbo housings, and reinforced oil feed lines.
entries.cta: Compare prices for 939 A4.000 JTDM engine

entries.title: 939 A2.000 1.6L Diesel
entries.description: Powers compact Mito (955) and Giulietta (940) models from 2009 to 2020. DPF saturation and EGR cooler cracking frequently lead to oil contamination and head gasket compromise. Specialist rebuilds feature upgraded stainless-steel EGR coolers, reinforced DPF mounting brackets, and revised coolant flow channels.
entries.cta: Get quotes for 939 A2.000 diesel engine replacement

entries.title: 939 A6.000 2.0L Diesel
entries.description: Fits modern Giulia (952) and Stelvio (949) platforms from 2016 onward. Common issues include high-pressure fuel rail fatigue and timing chain stretch from extended service intervals. Replacement engines are fitted with heavy-duty chain kits, upgraded fuel rail assemblies, and reinforced cylinder head dowels.
entries.cta: Find rebuilt 939 A6.000 specialist quotes

entries.title: 939 A3.000 2.4L Diesel
entries.description: Exclusive to the 159 (939), Brera (939), and Spider (939) between 2005 and 2011. Crankshaft main bearing wear and turbo bearing fatigue frequently occur past 110,000 miles. Quality rebuilds incorporate uprated tri-metal bearing shells, a baffled sump tray, and reinforced turbo oil return lines.
entries.cta: Request pricing for 939 A3.000 engine

entries.title: 937 A8.000 1.9L Diesel
entries.description: Powers the 147 (937), 156 (932), and GT (937) from 2000 to 2010. Dual-mass flywheel resonance and injector seat erosion cause excessive vibration and compression loss. Reconditioned replacements include a reinforced dual-mass flywheel, Viton injector sealing collars, and resurfaced cylinder heads.
entries.cta: Compare 937 A8.000 reconditioned engine costs

entries.title: 836 A3.000 2.2L Diesel
entries.description: Found in late-model Giulia (952) and Stelvio (949) from 2018 onward. AdBlue pump crystallisation and EGR valve sticking trigger limp mode and DPF damage. Specialist rebuilds feature upgraded SCR pump assemblies, revised EGR valve mechanisms, and updated thermal management software.
entries.cta: Get quotes for 836 A3.000 multi-jet engine

entries.title: 937 A1.000 1.8L Petrol
entries.description: Used in 156 (932) and GT (937) chassis from 1995 to 2007. Phased cam variator wear and timing belt tensioner failure commonly cause catastrophic valve-to-piston contact. Rebuilt units feature double-row variator assemblies, reinforced belt tensioners, and upgraded tensioner pulleys for extended intervals.
entries.cta: Find rebuilt 937 A1.000 Twin Spark engine

entries.title: 937 A2.000 2.0L Petrol
entries.description: Powers 147 (937), 156 (932), GT (937), and GTV (916) from 1997 to 2006. Piston ring sticking leads to heavy oil consumption, while ignition coil degradation causes cylinder misfires. Specialist recon engines include revised low-tension piston rings, upgraded coil packs, and refreshed valve guide seals.
entries.cta: Compare quotes for 937 A2.000 engine replacement

entries.title: 939 A5.000 2.2L Petrol
entries.description: Fitted to 159 (939), Brera (939), and Spider (939) from 2005 to 2010. Direct injection carbon buildup on intake valves and timing chain stretch are well-documented replacement triggers. Quality rebuilds address this with hydro-blasted intake ports, heavy-duty chain kits, and updated cam phasers.
entries.cta: Get quotes for 939 A5.000 JTS engine

entries.title: 932 B2.000 2.0L Petrol
entries.description: Late-production Twin Spark variant in 156 (932) and GT (937) from 2003 to 2007. Similar failure profile to early 2.0L units but with added sensitivity to fuel quality and ignition timing drift. Reconditioned blocks feature hardened valve seats, upgraded spark plug leads, and recalibrated engine management modules.
entries.cta: Check 932 B2.000 replacement engine pricing

entries.title: 932 A5.000 3.0L V6 Petrol
entries.description: Powers 166 (936) executive saloons from 1998 to 2005. Timing belt neglect and oil gallery blockage frequently result in bearing spin and sudden oil pressure loss. Specialist rebuilds include reinforced belt kits, upgraded high-volume oil pumps, and machined gallery cleanouts for optimal lubrication.
entries.cta: Request quotes for 932 A5.000 V6 engine

entries.title: 670 05 0436 2.9L Petrol
entries.description: The Ferrari-developed Bi-Turbo V6 in Giulia Quadrifoglio (952) and Stelvio Quadrifoglio (949) from 2016 onward. High lateral G-forces cause oil starvation during track use, accelerating rod bearing and crankshaft journal wear. Premium rebuilds feature forged connecting rods, a baffled dry-sump conversion, and uprated main bearings.
entries.cta: Compare specialist 670 05 0436 engine prices

entries.title: 936 A.000 3.2L Petrol
entries.description: Busso-derived V6 used in 159 (939), Brera (939), Spider (939), and GT (937) from 2005 to 2010. Head gasket failure under sustained heat and timing guide degradation are primary replacement drivers. Reconditioned units include multi-layer steel gaskets, metal composite chain guides, and reinforced thermostat housings.
entries.cta: Get quotes for 936 A.000 performance engine

entries.title: 670 05 2721 2.9L Petrol
entries.description: Updated iteration of the Quadrifoglio V6, found in 2019–2025 Giulia and Stelvio facelifts. Shares oil system vulnerabilities with the original 0436 variant but adds high-pressure injector tip failures. Rebuilt specifications incorporate revised injector nozzles, baffled oil pans, and upgraded crankshaft ventilation.
entries.cta: Find rebuilt 670 05 2721 Quadrifoglio engine

entries.title: 932 A2.000 2.5L Petrol
entries.description: Classic naturally aspirated V6 in 156 GTA (932) and 166 (936) from 1997 to 2005. Timing belt failure and oil pump drive wear commonly lead to complete valve train collapse. Quality rebuilds feature double-reinforced belt assemblies, high-flow pump drives, and resurfaced cylinder decks for optimal sealing.
entries.cta: Compare prices for 932 A2.000 Busso V6

engineYears.h2: Alfa Romeo Engines by Year – What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section maps every major Alfa Romeo engine change across the full UK model range - from the twin-spark era of the mid-1990s through to today's electrified Stellantis platforms - so owners can identify their exact engine by vehicle year and compare rebuilt engine prices from UK specialists.

years.preview: Twin Spark CF1 & World-First Common Rail Diesel Major Engine Family Introduction
years.description: The mid-1990s reshaped Alfa Romeo's engine story with the launch of the modern 16v Twin Spark petrol family and the world's first passenger-car common-rail diesel - both debut years that still define the brand's UK replacement market today.
keyChanges: 16v Twin Spark CF1 family launched on GTV and Spider (1995), replacing the aged Nord-based units
Alfa Romeo 156 introduces the 1.9 JTD in 1997 - the world's first production passenger car with common-rail diesel injection
2.4 JTD five-cylinder diesel introduced alongside the 1.9, providing a higher-output diesel option
Busso V6 (2.5 and 3.0) continues as the flagship naturally aspirated petrol across 155 and 164 runout
Euro 2 compliance shapes all new introductions from this period
mainEngines: AR16201 - 2.0 Twin Spark CF1 - 16v petrol (GTV, Spider, 155)
AR16201 / 1.6 & 1.8 CF1 - 1.6/1.8 Twin Spark - petrol (145, 146, 155, GTV, Spider)
AR32310 - Busso V6 2.5/3.0 - naturally aspirated petrol (155, 164, GTV)
knownFor: Twin Spark CF1 units revered for character, response, and high-revving nature - alloy cam covers mark early examples and are sought by enthusiasts
1.9 JTD a genuine engineering landmark: world-first common-rail diesel, widely praised for fuel economy and reliability at mileage when maintained
Busso V6 in NA form at peak reputation here - exceptional acoustics, smooth delivery, robust if serviced correctly
VVT phaser wear on Twin Spark units a known issue from 60,000+ miles; coil pack degradation also common on higher-mileage CF1 examples
years.ticker: Common Replacement Enquiries: Alfa Romeo 1.9 JTD replacement engine 156 - Alfa Romeo 2.0 Twin Spark engine rebuild - Alfa Romeo 156 diesel engine supply - Alfa Romeo GTV 2.0 TS engine
years.cta: Get quotes for 1995–1998 Alfa Romeo replacement engines from UK-verified specialists

years.preview: Twin Spark CF2, 147 Launch & JTD Expansion Major Reliability Revision
years.description: The late 1990s saw the 156 firmly establish the JTD diesel as Alfa's UK volume engine while the CF2 Twin Spark generation brought variable-length inlet manifolds and improved power delivery to the newly launched 147.
keyChanges: Twin Spark CF2 evolution introduces variable-length inlet manifold on 1.8 and 2.0 units, lifting power outputs
Alfa Romeo 147 launched (2000) bringing CF2 Twin Spark across the hatchback range
166 flagship introduced with 2.0 Twin Spark and Busso V6 3.0/3.2 options
1.9 JTD expands to 147 range in UK (formally from March 2003 introduction, engines aligned from 2001 build)
Euro 3 compliance drives ECU and catalytic converter updates across the petrol range
mainEngines: AR32310 - 1.6/1.8/2.0 Twin Spark CF2 - 16v petrol (147, 156, 166, GTV, Spider)
AR32501 - Busso V6 3.0/3.2 - 24v naturally aspirated petrol (166, GTV, Spider V6)
knownFor: 1.9 JTD 8v praised as one of Alfa's most durable engines - solid block, reliable injectors, capable of 150,000+ miles with timing belt adherence
CF2 Twin Spark delivers noticeably smoother throttle response than CF1; plastic rocker cover replaces alloy but retains mechanical character
Busso V6 3.2 24v in GTV/Spider considered the apex of the naturally aspirated era - audiophile-grade engine note, robust internals
166 body corrosion a separate concern but the powertrains themselves highly regarded by long-term owners
years.ticker: Common Replacement Enquiries: Alfa Romeo 147 1.9 JTD engine replacement - Alfa Romeo 156 2.0 Twin Spark engine - Alfa Romeo 166 V6 engine supply UK - Alfa Romeo 156 Sportwagon diesel engine
years.cta: Compare 1999–2002 Alfa Romeo engine prices - rebuilt units in stock from UK suppliers

years.preview: JTD MultiJet Upgrade & Twin Spark CF3 Era Major Engine Family Introduction
years.description: The introduction of the MultiJet multi-injection JTD system in 2003 was Alfa Romeo's diesel second act - delivering significantly better economy, emissions compliance, and refinement, while the CF3 Twin Spark entered its final chapter meeting Euro 3 with three catalysts.
keyChanges: 1.9 JTDm (MultiJet) replaces 8v JTD across 147 and 156 - multiple injection pulses per combustion cycle improve efficiency and reduce particulates
2.4 JTDm 20v five-cylinder upgraded to MultiJet injection for Euro 3/4 compliance
Twin Spark CF3 gains electronic throttle management and third catalytic converter for Euro 3
Alfa Romeo GT launched (2004) carrying 1.8 CF3 Twin Spark and 1.9 JTDm
2.0 JTS (Jet Thrust Stoichiometric) direct-injection petrol introduced as replacement for 2.0 Twin Spark in 156 and 156 Sportwagon
knownFor: 1.9 JTDm 8v widely regarded as one of the most reliable Alfa Romeo diesel units ever - robust, economical, tolerant of higher mileages
2.0 JTS attracted mixed reception: smoother than expected but lacking the Twin Spark's character, and early DI carbon build-up noted on intake valves
2.4 JTDm 20v five-cylinder delivers strong torque but swirl flap actuator failure is a documented weak point at 80,000–120,000 miles
CF3 Twin Spark criticised by some enthusiasts for reduced throttle feel versus CF1/CF2, but mechanically proven and parts-abundant
years.ticker: Common Replacement Enquiries: Alfa Romeo 1.9 JTDm engine replacement - Alfa Romeo 147 JTD engine supply - Alfa Romeo GT diesel engine - Alfa Romeo 2.4 JTD five cylinder engine
years.cta: Find a replacement 2003–2005 Alfa Romeo engine - verified rebuilt and reconditioned units available

years.preview: 159 / Brera Generation & 2.4 JTDm Peak Performance Landmark
years.description: The launch of the 159 and Brera in 2006 represented Alfa Romeo's most complete UK model range in years, introducing the 2.2 JTS direct petrol, the 3.2 V6 Q4 flagship, and the 2.4 JTDm five-cylinder at its commercial peak - while the MiTo's arrival in 2008 signalled the start of the small-car turbo era.
keyChanges: Alfa Romeo 159 and Brera launched (2006) with full engine range: 1.8/2.2 JTS petrol, 1.9/2.4 JTDm diesel, 3.2 V6
2.2 JTS four-cylinder direct injection petrol debuts as the standard-bearer petrol above the 1.8
3.2 V6 Q4 with six-speed automatic marks Alfa Romeo's performance flagship for the era
Alfa Romeo 8C Competizione launched (2007) with 4.7-litre Ferrari-derived V8 - a landmark performance engine
MiTo (2008) introduces the small 1.4 TB (turbocharged) and 1.6/1.9 JTDm to the supermini class
mainEngines: 939B4.000 - 3.2 V6 Q4 - naturally aspirated petrol (159, Brera)
knownFor: 2.4 JTDm five-cylinder delivers exceptional diesel torque but swirl flap failure remains the defining maintenance issue - a critical £300–£600 repair that catches neglected examples
1.9 JTDm 16v continues as Alfa's most dependable diesel; 159 Sportwagon examples are among the most sought replacement-engine enquiries in this era
3.2 V6 Q4 is an audiophile choice - strong, distinctive, but poor real-world mpg means examples are high mileage today
8C Competizione's Ferrari-sourced V8 is a collector's engine; parts and rebuild costs reflect its rarity
years.ticker: Common Replacement Enquiries: Alfa Romeo 159 1.9 JTDm engine replacement - Alfa Romeo 159 2.4 JTD engine supply - Alfa Romeo Brera diesel engine UK - Alfa Romeo 159 Sportwagon engine - Alfa Romeo MiTo 1.4 TB engine
years.cta: Get replacement engine quotes for your 2006–2009 Alfa Romeo - 159, Brera and MiTo specialists available

years.preview: Giulietta MultiAir Launch & Euro 5 Transition Major Engine Family Introduction Technology Introduction
years.description: The Giulietta's arrival in 2010 brought Alfa Romeo's most technically sophisticated petrol engine to date - the 1.4 TB MultiAir, with electrohydraulic variable valve actuation - marking a genuine engineering step change, while the 1.6 and 2.0 JTDm II diesels pushed into Euro 5 compliance across the brand.
keyChanges: Alfa Romeo Giulietta (2010) launches with 1.4 TB MultiAir and 1.75 TBi petrol, plus 1.6/2.0 JTDm II diesel
MultiAir electrohydraulic valve actuation technology introduced - eliminates throttle body for load control, reducing pumping losses and improving efficiency
1.75 TBi four-cylinder turbocharged petrol debuts in Giulietta and MiTo - 235hp flagship petrol
1.6 JTDm introduced as the efficient entry diesel across MiTo and Giulietta
Euro 5 compliance mandates DPF fitment across all diesel variants
MiTo updates align with Giulietta engines for platform consistency
mainEngines: 940A1.000 - 1.4 TB MultiAir - turbocharged petrol with variable valve actuation (Giulietta, MiTo)
955A2.000 - 1.4 TB MultiAir (revised) - improved solenoid (Giulietta, MiTo post-2012)
940B2.000 - 1.75 TBi - turbocharged four-cylinder petrol (Giulietta QV, MiTo QV)
knownFor: Early 940A1.000 MultiAir solenoid actuators have a documented failure rate from 60,000–80,000 miles - Stellantis issued TSB 12-008-20 and revised the solenoid pack in the post-2012 955A2.000 specification
1.75 TBi is compact and punchy - strong throttle response but oil consumption on high-mileage examples and timing chain stretch are known watch-points
1.6 JTDm rated for reliability and economy; DPF regeneration failures common in urban-only driving cycles
Giulietta MultiAir is the peak of Alfa's pre-Giorgio engineering: premium feel, genuine driver engagement, but maintenance-sensitive
years.ticker: Common Replacement Enquiries: Alfa Romeo Giulietta 1.4 MultiAir engine replacement - Alfa Romeo 1.75 TBi engine supply - Alfa Romeo Giulietta 2.0 JTDm diesel engine - Alfa Romeo MiTo 1.4 TB engine rebuild - Alfa Romeo MultiAir actuator failure engine replacement
years.cta: Compare Alfa Romeo Giulietta and MiTo replacement engine prices - 2010–2013 units from UK rebuilders

years.preview: 4C Mid-Engine Sports Car & Stable Euro 5 Lineup
years.description: The launch of the 4C (2013) brought a bespoke mid-mounted turbocharged four-cylinder into Alfa Romeo's model range for the first time in decades, while the broader lineup maintained its Euro 5 MultiAir and JTDm II engines without major architectural change.
keyChanges: Alfa Romeo 4C (2013) launched with a unique 1.75 turbocharged four-cylinder in a mid-engine, carbon-chassis two-seater
Giulietta updated with revised MultiAir mapping and improved solenoid hardware from TSB corrections
MiTo receives minor engine management updates for Euro 5 refinement
No new engine families introduced - a period of consolidation and reliability improvement
knownFor: 4C's 1.75 TBi in mid-engine application produces blistering performance-to-weight ratio - but specific 4C dry-sump tuning means parts are not fully interchangeable with Giulietta equivalents
Revised MultiAir solenoid from 2012 onward makes this period safer for used buyers - 2013+ Giulietta examples carry the improved actuator
2.0 JTDm II in Giulietta a strong touring diesel; high-mileage examples attract brisk replacement enquiry
MiTo begins a long slow decline in UK sales but engines remain well-supported by Stellantis parts network
years.cta: Find rebuilt 2013–2015 Alfa Romeo engines - 4C, Giulietta and MiTo covered

years.preview: Giorgio Platform, GME 2.0 Turbo & 2.9 V6 Biturbo Major Engine Family Introduction Performance Landmark
years.description: The Giulia and Stelvio launch in 2016 represented Alfa Romeo's most significant platform and powertrain investment in a generation - debuting the all-aluminium GME 2.0 turbo petrol, the 2.9-litre Ferrari-developed twin-turbocharged V6, and the Euro 6-compliant 2.2 JTDM II diesel on the all-new Giorgio rear-wheel-drive architecture.
keyChanges: Alfa Romeo Giulia (2016) and Stelvio (2017) launch on Giorgio platform with entirely new engine family
GME (Global Medium Engine) 2.0 Turbocharged petrol debuts in 200hp and 280hp outputs - all-aluminium, eight-speed auto
2.9-litre Ferrari-derived twin-turbocharged V6 bi-turbo introduced as Quadrifoglio flagship - 510hp in Giulia QV, 520hp in Stelvio QV
2.2 JTDM II diesel (Euro 6) replaces all previous diesel units in Giulia and Stelvio - 150hp and 180hp variants
Timing chain adopted on 2.2 JTDM II instead of belt - significant long-term maintenance change
mainEngines: 552S8.000 - 2.2 JTDM II - Euro 6 diesel, chain-driven (Giulia, Stelvio)
690T1A - 2.9 V6 Bi-Turbo - twin-turbocharged petrol, 510–520hp (Giulia QV, Stelvio QV)
knownFor: GME 2.0 turbo praised for punchy power delivery and robust internals - substantially more reliable feel than the MultiAir predecessor
2.9 V6 Bi-Turbo is a collector-grade performance engine: Ferrari DNA, extraordinary sound, and a strong reliability track record relative to its power output
2.2 JTDM II's timing chain is a major advantage over belt-driven predecessors; EGR coking in urban cycles remains the principal maintenance watch-point
Giulia QV and Stelvio QV generate strong replacement enquiries as performance examples accumulate track miles
years.ticker: Common Replacement Enquiries: Alfa Romeo Giulia 2.0 turbo engine replacement - Alfa Romeo Stelvio 2.2 diesel engine supply - Alfa Romeo Giulia Quadrifoglio V6 engine - Alfa Romeo 2.9 bi-turbo V6 engine UK - Alfa Romeo Stelvio engine rebuild
years.cta: Get quotes for 2016–2019 Alfa Romeo Giulia and Stelvio replacement engines - GME, V6 and 2.2 JTDM II specialists

years.preview: Euro 6d Compliance & Mild Hybrid Introduction Technology Introduction
years.description: Euro 6d-TEMP and then full Euro 6d drove significant calibration and hardware changes across the Giulia and Stelvio range, while the Tonale concept previewed Alfa Romeo's first plug-in hybrid powertrain - and the MiTo reached the end of its production run.
keyChanges: Euro 6d-TEMP compliance introduces AdBlue SCR system on 2.2 JTDM II diesel variants (Giulia, Stelvio)
GME 2.0 petrol recalibrated for full Euro 6d RDE cycle - revised fuel maps and particulate filtering
MiTo production ends (2018 final sales, cleared in UK to 2020) - 1.4 TB MultiAir and 1.4 TB engines exit the new car market
Tonale concept shown (2019), signalling incoming PHEV strategy
Giulietta production ends 2020 in European markets - JTDm and MultiAir engines enter pure aftermarket phase
mainEngines: 552S8.000 - 2.2 JTDM II (Euro 6d) - updated SCR diesel (Giulia, Stelvio)
GME 2.0 - 2.0 Turbocharged petrol (Euro 6d) - revised calibration (Giulia, Stelvio)
690T1A - 2.9 V6 Bi-Turbo - continued (Giulia QV, Stelvio QV)
940A1.000 / 955A2.000 - 1.4 TB MultiAir - final UK stock clearance (MiTo, Giulietta runout)
knownFor: AdBlue SCR systems add a new potential failure point - dosing unit blockage and NOx sensor faults are emerging concerns on higher-mileage 2020+ JTDM II examples
GME 2.0 petrol showing strong long-term durability record - among the more robust modern turbocharged four-cylinders in its displacement class
Giulietta and MiTo MultiAir engines now entering replacement-demand volume phase as mileages build and original units require rebuild
Euro 6d RDE calibration on JTDM II creates DPF regeneration sensitivity in short-journey driving patterns
years.ticker: Common Replacement Enquiries: Alfa Romeo Giulia diesel engine Euro 6 replacement - Alfa Romeo Stelvio 2.2 JTDm II engine supply - Alfa Romeo Giulietta engine replacement 2020 - Alfa Romeo MiTo engine rebuild UK
years.cta: Compare Euro 6d-era Alfa Romeo replacement engine quotes - Giulia and Stelvio diesel and petrol units

years.preview: Tonale PHEV, Junior EV & Electrified Era Technology Introduction
years.description: The Tonale (2022) brought Alfa Romeo's first production plug-in hybrid powertrain to market, pairing a 1.3-litre turbocharged MultiAir-derived petrol engine with a rear electric motor - while the Junior (2024) introduced the brand's first fully electric drivetrain to UK buyers.
keyChanges: Alfa Romeo Tonale launched with 1.5 mild-hybrid petrol (48V) and 1.3 PHEV (plug-in hybrid, rear e-motor)
Junior (2024) arrives as Alfa Romeo's first BEV for UK market - electric motor only, no combustion engine
2.2 JTDM II diesel continues in Giulia and Stelvio but confirmed for phase-out ahead of EU 2035 ICE regulations
GME 2.0 petrol continues in Giulia, Stelvio with minor emissions refinements
Stellantis electrification roadmap moves Alfa Romeo toward 100% electrified lineup by 2027
mainEngines: 552G7.000 - 1.3 TB MultiAir PHEV - turbocharged petrol + rear e-motor (Tonale PHEV)
552S8.000 - 2.2 JTDM II - Euro 6d diesel (Giulia, Stelvio - continued)
knownFor: Tonale 1.3 PHEV partners the MultiAir-derived combustion unit with a smooth rear e-motor integration - real-world EV range modest but WLTP figures compliant
1.5 MHEV 48v mild-hybrid is the volume choice for Tonale: responsive and frugal but 48V belt-starter-generator adds a new service consideration

Junior's BEV drivetrain represents a clean break from Alfa's combustion heritage - no engine replacement demand by definition, but battery health monitoring will become the equivalent aftermarket concern
GME 2.0 and 2.2 JTDM II in Giulia and Stelvio remain strong sellers for UK specialists as these platforms enter their highest-mileage years
years.ticker: Common Replacement Enquiries: Alfa Romeo Tonale engine replacement - Alfa Romeo Giulia 2.0 turbo high mileage engine - Alfa Romeo Stelvio 2.2 diesel replacement engine - Alfa Romeo PHEV engine supply UK
years.cta: Get quotes for 2022-onwards Alfa Romeo Tonale, Giulia and Stelvio replacement engines - current-spec units available from UK specialists

engineYears.closing: Not sure which year your Alfa Romeo was built? Enter your registration number and we'll identify the exact engine, model year, and compatible replacement options instantly - all backed by a minimum 12-month warranty from UK Alfa Romeo specialists.

faq.h2: Alfa Romeo Engine Replacement – Frequently Asked Questions
faq.intro: ** Quick, honest answers to the most common questions Alfa Romeo owners ask before comparing engine replacement prices and finding a UK specialist.

items.question: How much does a Alfa Romeo engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: Reconditioned Alfa Romeo engines range from £1,800–£2,800 for compact 1.3/1.4L units (Mito/Giulietta) to £4,500–£6,500 for performance 2.9L V6 Quadrifoglio rebuilds. Specialist fitting typically adds £600–£1,200, while main dealers may charge double. We always recommend confirming whether VAT, delivery and fluids are included before committing.
items.cta: Compare Alfa Romeo engine replacement prices now to see live quotes from UK specialists

items.question: What affects the cost of a Alfa Romeo engine replacement?
items.answer: Four key factors drive pricing: engine displacement (1.4L vs 2.9L V6), condition (used/recon/rebuilt), vehicle complexity (Giulietta vs AWD Stelvio), and whether you choose supply-only or supply-and-fit. Location matters too — London and South East labour rates run 20–30% higher. Our comparison tool provides full transparency across all variables so you see exactly what you're paying for.
items.cta: Get quotes from UK Alfa Romeo engine specialists today

items.question: Is it worth replacing the engine in my Alfa Romeo?
items.answer: In most cases, yes — especially if the chassis, gearbox and interior remain in good condition. A rebuilt Alfa Romeo engine with upgraded timing components or reinforced turbo feeds often outlasts the original factory unit. With comparable Giulietta or Giulia models commanding £12k–£20k on the used market, engine replacement is frequently the smarter financial choice.
items.cta: Find reconditioned Alfa Romeo engines with comprehensive warranty cover

items.question: What's included in a Alfa Romeo engine supply and fit service?
items.answer: Supply-and-fit covers sourcing your exact engine code, professional removal of the failed unit, installation of the replacement, fresh fluids and filters, a workmanship warranty, responsible disposal of the old engine, and a post-fit test drive. Specialist fitting typically costs £600–£1,200 depending on model complexity. Always confirm whether the quote includes VAT and any necessary ECU coding.
items.cta: Compare Alfa Romeo engine supply and fit deals now

items.question: How long does a Alfa Romeo engine replacement take?
items.answer: For common models like Giulietta 1.4 MultiAir or 2.0 JTDM, expect 3–5 working days from engine arrival to road-ready. More complex platforms such as Giulia Quadrifoglio or AWD Stelvio may require 5–7 days due to additional calibration steps. The biggest variable is engine availability — our network stocks high-demand codes like 552 73 835 and 940A2.000 for faster turnaround.
items.cta: Ask suppliers about lead times when requesting your quote

items.question: Do reconditioned Alfa Romeo engines come with warranty?
items.answer: Yes — every reconditioned Alfa Romeo engine supplied through our network carries a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty as standard. Extended 24-month coverage is often available on fully rebuilt or remanufactured units. Warranty typically covers major internals including crankshaft, pistons, timing chain assembly and oil pump, but always confirm exact terms directly with your chosen supplier before proceeding.
items.cta: Find Alfa Romeo engine warranty options with instant quotes

items.question: Should I choose a used or reconditioned Alfa Romeo engine?
items.answer: Used Alfa Romeo engines (£800–£2,000) suit tight budgets or vehicles you plan to sell soon. Reconditioned units (£2,000–£4,500) include new seals, bearings and upgraded components addressing known failure points, plus warranty protection and 100k+ mile potential. For failure-prone families like 1.4 MultiAir or 2.0 JTDM, we strongly recommend reconditioned with upgrades for long-term peace of mind.
items.cta: Compare used vs recon Alfa Romeo engine prices today

items.question: Where can I find Alfa Romeo engine specialists near me?
items.answer: EngineMarket connects you with a UK-wide network of Alfa Romeo-trained specialists. Regional hubs in the Midlands, North West and South East hold particularly strong concentrations of brand expertise. Simply enter your registration to match with nearby suppliers who stock your specific engine code and offer fitting services. Nationwide delivery is available if no local specialist has your unit in stock.
items.cta: Get quotes from Alfa Romeo engine specialists near you now

items.question: What are the most common Alfa Romeo engine failures?
items.answer: The 1.4 TB MultiAir suffers plastic timing guide disintegration (Giulietta/Mito); 2.0/2.2 JTDM units experience turbo oil feed fractures and injector drift (Giulia/Stelvio); Busso V6 engines face oil pump drive gear shear (156/147/GTV); early GME T4 units had tensioner weakness and PCV sludge. These failures rarely allow preventative repair — replacement becomes the most reliable outcome.
items.cta: Compare prices for replacement Alfa Romeo engines affected by these common issues

items.question: How can I get the best price on a Alfa Romeo engine?
items.answer: Always compare multiple quotes — pricing can vary by £1,000+ on identical engine codes like 940A2.000 or 552 73 835. Consider used engines for tight budgets, but choose reconditioned with upgraded components for long-term ownership of failure-prone models. Ask about supply-only pricing if you have a trusted independent garage, and always verify whether fitting, delivery and VAT are included in the quoted figure.
items.cta: Use our free comparison tool to find the best price Alfa Romeo engine deals
